title: Talent Assessment | Recruitment Assessment Tool | SHL
content: World-Class Talent Assessments and Skill Tests Evaluate candidate fit, readiness, and potential with an unmatched portfolio of personality tests, skills assessments, and job simulations. Speak With Our Team Make Wise Talent Decisions Evaluate job-related behaviors, skills, and competencies with patented assessments developed by our 300+ people scientists to hire high-quality talent. Drive Hiring Efficiency Our assessments provide actionable, timely insights to accelerate hiring while driving objectivity and fairness in your people decisions. Delight Your Candidates SHL prioritizes candidate experiences to ensure you engage and excite candidates with interactive, realistic assessments and job simulations. Our Unmatched Portfolio of Assessments Behavioral Assessments Match each candidate’s behavioral fit with your role, to boost quality of hire and engagement. Discover Behavioral Assessments Virtual Assessment & Development Centers Our end-to-end digital assessment and development centers provide the most efficient and accurate way of identifying potential from anywhere in the world. Discover Virtual Assessment & Development Centers Personality Assessments Improve decisions with science-backed personality tests like OPQ that reveal individual preferences, motivations, and remote-work capability. Discover Personality Assessments Cognitive Assessments Measure candidates’ potential to learn, adapt, and perform in any open role with Verify, the interactive cognitive assessment. Discover Cognitive Assessments Skills and Simulations Recruit top candidates at scale with skills assessments and simulation tests that measure practical application and knowledge. Discover Skills & Simulations Job Focused Assessments Identify best-fit candidates with focused, comprehensive online assessments that measure job-specific competencies. Discovers JFAs Frequently Asked Questions for SHL Assessments What is the SHL OPQ? The Occupational Personality Questionnaire (OPQ) is SHL's flagship personality assessment, widely used to predict how a candidate's preferred personal style impacts their workplace behavior. It provides insights into an individual's potential by matching their preferences to role requirements and is backed by science with strong predictive accuracy. Are SHL assessments reliable? SHL's adherence to legal and scientific standards ensures that its assessments are developed and validated to be fair, reliable, and appropriate for their intended purposes in selection processes. The reliability of SHL assessments is reviewed during test development as well as a part of ongoing test maintenance practices. We document the reliability of our assessments (e.g., through test-retest and internal consistency methods) in our technical manuals. What are psychometric assessments? Psychometric assessments are standardized tests designed to assess candidates' cognitive abilities, personality traits, job-related skills and behavioral styles. They help organizations understand how candidates may perform in specific roles. SHL assessments are scientifically developed tools built on psychometric principles, ensuring they are reliable, valid, and suitable for making informed talent decisions. Why should I use psychometric assessments? Psychometric assessments offer objective and reliable insights into candidates' individual differences such as skills, abilities, personality traits, and potential, enabling organizations to make informed talent decisions. They help ensure a strong fit for specific roles and promote fairness by reducing bias. When applied consistently throughout the talent lifecycle, these assessments provide valuable data for strategic talent decisions, enhancing organizational agility and maximizing workforce effectiveness. Can psychometric assessments be tailored for an organization? Yes, many providers, including SHL, can offer fully customizable assessments that align with your organization's specific competencies, values, and culture so that you get exactly what you need out of your talent processes. Is it better to use psychometric assessments or AI to collect talent data? While AI-inferred talent data may offer some value, it is often less reliable and may introduce inaccuracies. Psychometric assessments, on the other hand, provide a more objective and scientifically validated understanding of what a candidate can do. In practice, AI-inferred data might be combined with psychometric data, but for robust and defensible decision-making, psychometric tools offer greater reliability and value. Are SHL assessments valid? SHL's adherence to legal and scientific standards ensures that its assessments are developed and validated to be fair, reliable, and appropriate for their intended purposes in selection processes. Information and evidence regarding the content, construct, and criterion-related validity of our assessments is documented in our technical manuals. However, the validity of these assessments depends on their proper use, which is beyond our control. We advise users to adhere to best practices to ensure accurate and fair outcomes. Are SHL assessments fair? SHL's adherence to legal and scientific standards ensures that its assessments are developed and validated to be fair, reliable, and appropriate for their intended purposes in selection processes. Our technical manuals summarize the analyses conducted to support the use of our assessments across and within protected groups. title: Global Skills Assessment | Measure 96 Workplace Skills | SHL
content: SHL Global Skills Assessment One Assessment, Every Talent Decision 96 behavioral skills measured in 15 minutes. Objective, consistent skills data you can use from hiring decisions to development conversations to internal mobility. Speak to an Expert A Clear Picture of Workplace Skills Across People and Teams The SHL Global Skills Assessment gives organizations a consistent, science‑based view of behavioral skills across candidates and employees. It provides a shared language for understanding strengths, gaps, and the behaviors that drive performance. Because it’s built on one universal framework , the assessment creates a unified picture of skills across your organization. You can compare people fairly, understand capability at scale, and build a stronger foundation for development, mobility, and long‑term workforce planning. Bring Objective Skills Insight into Every Talent Decision With one consistent framework before and after hire, you can use the SHL Global Skills Assessment in hiring, across your workforce, or both. Embed in Hiring Include the SHL Global Skills Assessment in role-specific hiring to identify your top candidates. Reuse their skills profiles at onboarding to guide targeted development from day one. Develop Your Workforce Give the SHL Global Skills Assessment directly to employees. In 15 minutes per person, you gain organization-wide visibility into capabilities through detailed reporting. Inside the SHL Global Skills Assessment Measure what matters The assessment measures all 96 skills in SHL’s Universal Competency Framework, giving you a complete view of the workplace behaviors that drive performance across roles. Gather meaningful data In around 15 minutes, individuals compare short behavioral statements and select the ones most like them. This simple, forced‑choice format captures authentic workplace behavior while reducing response bias. Deliver actionable insight The assessment produces a range of reports tailored to your use case. Individuals receive development insights they can use for onboarding or growth. HR teams can view results for single candidates or aggregated groups, and—when used for selection—generate structured interview guides or role‑aligned talent profiles. Identify who can grow Alongside current strengths and gaps, the Reskilling Potential add‑on shows who has the willingness and ability to successfully upskill. This gives you a clearer view of who can grow into future roles, projects, and opportunities. What Makes the SHL Global Skills Assessment Different? Globally normed Normed with global samples representative across job levels and geographies to enable consistent comparisons EEOC compliant Built to support fair practices grounded in EEOC guidance for equal opportunity Legally defensible Make decisions you can defend because you're using validated methods with documented job relevance See AI Readiness in the Skills You Already Measure The Global Skills Assessment gives you a complete picture of your workforce’s behavioral skills. Our SHL AI Readiness Profile applies a focused lens to that data, revealing who’s ready to work effectively in an AI-powered environment and where to build capability next. Learn more about AI Readiness SHL Global Skills Assessment Frequently Asked Questions We already have our own competency framework. Why would we use the SHL Universal Competency Framework (UCF)? Most organizations have multiple frameworks—one for hiring, another for performance, a third for leadership. The UCF gives you a single, validated framework that works across all of them. You can map your existing frameworks to the UCF (we help with this), which means you finally get consistent, comparable data. Plus, the UCF’s Skills Taxonomy is normed globally and mapped to 600+ O*NET profiles and 55,000 job titles, something custom frameworks can't match. How does this help with upskilling and reskilling? The GSA reveals individual strengths and skills gaps, making it clear where development is needed. It also identifies employees with high adaptability and ability to learn, those most ready to reskill into new roles as business needs evolve. This turns skills data into targeted development actions at individual, team, and organizational levels. How does skills data from hiring get used later in development or mobility? Skills data collected during hiring remain accessible throughout the employee lifecycle. The same objective profile that informed the hiring decision becomes the foundation for onboarding conversations, development planning, and internal movement—no repeat assessment required. Is there evidence the GSA predicts performance? Yes. 15+ independent validation studies across industries show GSA composites consistently predict supervisor-rated performance and productivity. What does "globally normed" mean for my organization? Global norms allow comparison across job levels, roles, and geographies using the same benchmark—whether you're assessing 50 people in one location or 50,000 across a global workforce. Related Solutions Hiring Solutions Make smarter hiring decisions with skills data that you can carry forward through the employee lifecycle. Learn More Skills Development Uncover skills gaps and build targeted development plans that align to business objectives. Learn More Talent Mobility Use objective skills data to match employees to the right internal opportunities, and vice versa. Learn More

title: Universal Competency Framework (UCF) | SHL
content: SHL Behavioral Assessments Universal Competency Framework (UCF) Learn how the UCF can help you understand your people. What Are Competencies? Competencies are ‘sets of behaviors that are instrumental in the delivery of desired results’ (Bartram et al, 2002). In the business context they are behaviors that support the attainment of objectives within the context of that specific organization’s strategy. Competencies are sometimes also called ‘capabilities’ but irrespective of the term used, the focus is on ‘behaviors’ not technical knowledge. What is the Universal Competency Framework? The Universal Competency Framework (UCF) provides a rational, consistent, and practical basis for understanding people’s behaviors at work and their likelihood of succeeding in certain roles and environments. We constructed the UCF from our wide-reaching program of research on workplace behaviors, so it can be used to build competency models for all types of jobs or to map existing models onto a universal set of competency components. We also provide a full suite of talent management tools and content to support the UCF including APTA™, our patented, customizable personnel psychological testing tool for directly measuring the UCF competencies most important for success in a role. Additional tools include questionnaire items, behavioral anchors, interview questions, and assessment exercises for employee selection and development. These provide the source materials for building assessment programs. Most importantly, each competency component is linked to our portfolio of assessments . How Universal Competency Framework Helps Organizations Achieve Their Goals A competency framework allows organizations to align individual behaviors to group or corporate goals. It specifies the requirements for good performance in a given role, thereby facilitating end-to-end talent management practices, from identifying job-relevant competencies to engaging talent assessment tools to managing performance. The competency framework can help you to easily understand the areas of strength and improvement within the whole organization across various departments and locations. Why Competency Frameworks Organizations using a consistent competency model across talent acquisition, learning performance management and succession processes are five times as likely to achieve best-in-class performance as those that do not. Aberdeen Strategy and Research Group The framework incorporates a model of performance at work that defines the relationships between competency potential, competency requirements and competencies themselves. The Universal Competency Framework, White Paper 2012 Read White Paper Universal Competency Framework (UCF) Structure of the Universal Competency Framework (UCF) The UCF is defined in terms of a 3-tier structure: 8 general competency factors at the highest level. 20 competency dimensions further explaining the general factors. 96 skills at the most granular level. Statistical analysis was progressed to inform this structure and to ensure the competencies at all levels are discrete. Benefits of the Universal Competency Framework (UCF) Defines critical behaviors for any role, Is firmly linked to observable behaviors, Is built on empirical evidence and extensive scientific research highlighting the most valid predictors of performance across roles, Provides a common framework to evaluate behaviors, potential, and performance, Has levels of behaviors to allow implementation across all levels and functions, and Provides consistent measurement that enables meaningful validation. Bringing Universal Competency Framework to the Next Level Our Science team is constantly innovating and pushing the boundaries of what is deemed possible. Their latest innovation is Universal Competency Assessment that can measure all 96 components of Universal Competency Framework in just 15 minutes! Learn more about their work in our new innovation powerhouse, SHL Labs . A Guide to SHL’s Universal Competency Framework (UCF) Science, Structure and Best Practices Learn the science and structure behind the UCF framework and how organizations can use it to map all job requirements all across levels, job families, and roles-together with example applications. The guide also includes best practices to thoughtfully implement, or review, competency frameworks, with seven key questions and practical tips to put them into action. title: Realistic Job and Culture Previews (RJP) | SHL
content: SHL Realistic Job Previews Preview roles with scenario-based quizzes that help candidates better understand the role before applying. Speak With Our Team Get candidate commitment from the start. Realistic job previews ask a short set of task and culture-based questions to help candidates self-assess their fit with this role and with your company. This helps you engage best-fit applicants, and allow them to consciously opt-in. Present each opportunity as a complete picture. RJPs are packed with immersive, branded multimedia, including employee video endorsements and animation, which create appealing and contextual views of your business. Bolster your candidate advocacy with powerful, repeatable promotion. Increase the efficiency of your recruitment. RJP’s instant feedback is used for guidance, not overall scoring. By empowering applicants to opt out pre-application, you reduce the risks of shortlisting candidates ill-fitted to your business and save applicants and recruiters valuable time. Understand How Our Assessments Work Preview roles and fit with realistic job previews. Realistic Job and Culture Previews (RJP) SHL’s experts develop scenarios that best explain and showcase each job opportunity. Realistic Job and Culture Previews (RJP) feature customized questions and interactive multimedia, fully branded to your brand. Assessing Candidate Fit Candidates are immersed in three or four (typically) realistic scenarios, choosing the response they feel is most appropriate to them. Candidate Feedback Candidates receive immediate feedback on their suitability. If they believe they are a good fit, they can proceed to the next stage of their hiring journey with your company. title: Situational Judgment Tests (SJT) | SHL
content: SHL Behavioral Assessments Situational Judgement Tests Match candidates’ behavioral fit to your roles with immersive, interactive scenarios and effectively scale screening. Speak With Our Team SHL Insights 72% of candidates were more likely to accept an offer from a company using multimedia for their online recruitment process than just text. Measure candidates’ behavioral fit. Situational Judgement Tests (SJTs) score candidate responses to a typical work situation using consistent, objective behavioral science, expertly tailored to your business. Reliably find and secure best-fit candidates, especially when experience is hard to assess. Screen at scale with purpose-built assessments. SHL's Situational Judgement Tests remove the burden of reviewing countless CVs. They measure behaviors, not tasks, so they’re easy to adjust as your organization develops. Save time with a cost-effective, universal, futureproof approach to screening. Establish a psychological contract with candidates. This short assessment is packed with multimedia, from 3D animations to interactive videos that showcase the role and your company culture. Matchmaking works both ways—this is how you win candidates’ commitment to your process. Understand How Our Assessments Work Match candidates’ behavioral fit to your roles with a situational judgment test. These tests are typically administered early in the assessment process, they provide candidates with a fully-branded introduction to your business. Evaluating Candidates' Judgement Candidates complete role and culture-based brief scenarios tailored to specific roles or drawn from SHL's bank for quick deployment. Evaluating Behavioral Fit Our science evaluates each candidate's behavioral fit to your role using our Universal Competency Framework , the industry’s leading assessment of behavioral competency. Streamline Candidate Selection Streamline candidate selection with Situational Judgement Tests (SJTs) for high-quality hires. SJTs are available within most SHL talent acquisition solutions. title: Cognitive Ability Tests | Cognitive Assessments | SHL
content: SHL Cognitive Assessments Measure potential to learn, adapt, and perform in any role with SHL Verify Interactive cognitive assessments. Speak with our Team SHL Insights 2500+ clients use our cognitive assessments as part of their talent acquisition strategies. Understand candidates’ learning potential and adaptability. SHL combines interactive numerical, deductive reasoning, and reasoning ability assessments with data gathered from decades of global experience. Gain insight into each candidate’s ability to learn—and, combined with behavioral assessments, their willingness to try. Quickly and accurately assess candidates at scale. SHL’s cognitive assessments are automated, remotely proctored, and ask fewer questions thanks to our unique partial-scoring methodology. Assess high candidate volumes faster and more reliably, so your teams can make confident, efficient hiring decisions. Provide all candidates with truly engaging experiences. Our immersive assessments upgrade button clicking with “drag-and-drop" and gamified interactions without compromising professional assessment standards. This helps your teams keep applicants engaged, while meeting their expectation of being taken seriously with job-relevant, real-world tasks. Understand candidate learning potential before making hiring decisions. SHL's Cognitive Assessments Our pre-employment cognitive assessment starts by asking candidates a series of interactive questions organized around a central principle linked to something they are likely to encounter in the role. SHL Verify G+ Test Measures general mental ability , or G, and effectively measures candidates’ problem-solving and critical reasoning capabilities. SHL Verify Deductive Reasoning Measures candidates’ ability to draw logical conclusions and complete scenarios based on the information provided. Tasks include reading statements, then determining which subsequent options cannot be true or must be true. SHL Verify Interactive – Inductive Reasoning Measures candidates’ ability to draw inferences from, and understand relationships between, various concepts. Tasks include working out the pattern in a set of images and identifying the correct next image. SHL Verify Interactive – Numerical Reasoning Measures candidates’ ability to work with numbers and use appropriate mathematics in different situations. Tasks include solving word problems, performing numerical calculations, and interpreting charts and graphs. Our Assessment Science SHL's Verify Interactive delivers precise scores indicating candidates' cognitive ability. These scores, along with other SHL talent acquisition data such as behavior and personality assessments, culminate in a well-rounded job score for each candidate. SHL's cognitive assessments aim to empower your teams to make fair and efficient talent choices, leading to business success. Hear from Our Customers SHL is helping us to find top talent to create and manage the professional, motivated, and resilient teams we need to deliver excellent customer service. We use SHL assessments to match the competency profiles of our managers and specialists with business needs and thereby focus our development activities. title: Skills Assessment Test | Skills Assessment Tool | SHL
content: SHL Skills Assessments and Job Simulations Measure practical application and knowledge virtually to scale your search for highly skilled candidates. Speak With Our Team Accurately assess knowledge, concepts, and practical application. Reliably progress skilled candidates to the next stage of your hiring process. Increase reach virtually and save interviewers’ time. Our skills assessment tests are automated, remotely proctored, and can assess hundreds of applicants remotely and simultaneously. Make your hiring process accessible to more candidates—and let hiring managers concentrate on converting talent. Help candidates demonstrate their skills. title: SHL SVAR | Spoken Language Assessment for Hiring
content: Language Evaluation SVAR Assessment: Measure Real Spoken Language, at Scale Assess genuine language skills across speaking, vocabulary, grammar, listening and comprehension — giving you a reliable signal you can trust in high-volume hiring. Book a Demo A Smarter Way to Assess Spoken Language SHL SVAR is our automated spoken-language assessment designed to measure real communication ability. It evaluates how candidates speak and converse to give you a clear, evidence‑based view of workplace‑ready communication. SVAR supports 20+ languages and 7 accents , works on mobile and web , and takes just 12–15 minutes to complete. AI‑powered scoring delivers fast, consistent results so hiring teams can assess spoken‑language capability at scale. What does SHL SVAR measure? SHL SVAR measures the essentials of spoken communication in one quick assessment; how clearly candidates speak, their pronunciation and fluency, how well they understand and respond to information, and how accurately they use vocabulary and grammar. And because it captures spontaneous, real-time speech, you get a genuine view of a candidate's spoken ability rather than a rehearsed response. Automated scoring delivers fast, consistent results, helping you screen high-volume and multilingual talent pools with confidence. How SHL SVAR Works It’s designed to be simple for candidates and efficient for hiring teams. Step 1: Candidates listen to a sentence, prompt, or spoken information. Step 2: They repeat or respond in their own words. Step 3: AI models analyze pronunciation, fluency, grammar, vocabulary, and comprehension. Step 4: Hiring teams receive a clear report showing performance across key language metrics. The result: a fast, transparent and evidence‑based view of spoken language capability. Built with Rigor SHL SVAR combines globally recognized language standards with AI-driven speech analysis to deliver reliable, scalable spoken language assessment. Developed using globally sourced Common European Framework (CEFR)-aligned data and rated by CEFR experts Uses AI-driven speech and language models Validated on a diverse datasets with strong AI-to-expert correlation Informed by multiple validation studies designed to support inclusivity and fairness What This Means for Your Business Enhanced customer satisfaction Clear communication leads to better experiences and stronger relationships Improved team collaboration Shared language standards support smoother internal and external communication Reduced training costs Accurate assessment means hiring candidates with the right language skills from day one Global standardization Consistent language standards across international operations Who’s It For? SHL SVAR is built for roles where spoken communication directly impacts performance, customer experience, and service quality. Common use cases include: Customer service and contact centres Technical support and helpdesks Telesales and inside sales Front‑office and client‑facing roles Multilingual or global hiring programmes SHL SVAR helps teams quickly identify candidates who can communicate clearly, confidently, and effectively from day one. Find candidates at scale that will quickly make an impact and grow with your organization. title: Job-Focused Assessments | Skills Assessments | SHL
content: Job-Focused Assessments Skills Assessments that Predict On-the-job Success Measure the skills that matter for success in any role. Speak to Our Experts What are Job-Focused Assessments? SHL’s Job-Focused Assessments predict job success by evaluating key hard and soft skills tailored to specific roles. Applicants take a short, engaging assessment relevant to the job and you gain a comprehensive view of candidates' role readiness, team fit and future potential. Skills Assessments to Predict On-the-Job Success Fast and accurate measurement of job-relevant skills using patented Apta technology. Strong Prediction of Success Get a well-rounded view of each candidate’s level of potential, readiness, and fit through one single assessment. Job-Focused Assessments utilize Apta, a direct measure of observable competency behaviors linked to performance, which has been proven to reduce faking and impression management. Engaging and Job-Relevant Attract best-fit talent with skills assessments that are directly related to the role they are applying for. Target Key Outcomes for Specific Roles Measure the skills and competencies that matter most to any specific job or role level. Pre-packaged assessments to ensure quick deployment. Enable Fair Decision-Making Select candidates based on the skills they possess or ability and desire to grow, without relying on past experience and formal qualifications. Skills Assessment for Any Role Unlock the full potential of your hiring process through SHL’s hiring solution packages, which all include job-focused assessments. Evaluate the essential skills for any role and beyond, while providing candidates with a memorable and intuitive experience.
